context_note: New Zealand vs Egypt, WC2026 Group Stage (Group G). EGY 61.17% FTR; NZL 14.93%. Draw 23.54% — just below RULE8 threshold (25%), no RULE8. Not extreme favorite — no RULE7 (61.17% < 86%). NZL 14.93% = deep underdog → RULE15 applies to NZL primary forwards (Ben Waine, Elijah Just). Prior data: NZL 2-2 Iran (NZL 8 fouls, 0 YC, 0 offsides, 1 corner, 8 SOT, 14 shots, 51.5% poss); EGY 1-1 Belgium (EGY 15 fouls, 2 YC, 1 offside, 7 corners, 3 SOT, 14 shots, 46.4% poss). KEY FINDING: EGY had 7 corners vs Belgium while NZL had 1 corner vs Iran — massive asymmetry. NZL shot-to-SOT conversion rate was 57% (8/14) vs Egypt's 21% (3/14). Trezeguet was a SUBSTITUTE vs Belgium (GD1 bench role). Ben Waine is Port Vale (League One) striker.
RULE8_note: Draw 23.54% is just below 25% threshold. Match is fairly competitive (Egypt ~61%, NZL ~15%, Draw ~24%). No formal RULE8 fire but the close-but-below 25% draw suggests some moderate-scoring tendencies consistent with RULE8 adjacent matches. Watch for low-goal outcomes (OU25 Under=53.63% supports ≤2 goals majority of the time).

derivation_notes.EGY_goals: From EGY OU05 Over=83.34%: -ln(0.1666)=1.7922. Check: EGY OU15 Over model=0.5348 vs market=0.5334 ✓
derivation_notes.NZL_goals: From NZL OU05 Over=54.22%: -ln(0.4578)=0.7813. Check: NZL OU15 Over model=0.1845 vs market=0.1874 ✓
derivation_notes.total_check: P(≥3|2.5735)=0.4749 vs market OU25 Over=0.463 — close ✓
derivation_notes.SOT_implied_lines: Smarkets created markets: egypt-OU5.5-SOT (143912760) and new-zealand-OU2.5-SOT (143912709) — illiquid but lines imply λ_EGY_SOT≈5.5 and λ_NZL_SOT≈2.5. These lines are set by Smarkets near expected value (verified pattern from prior matches). CRITICAL for Q3 and Q9.

smarkets_prices.illiquid_markets: cards_OU25/35/45, penalty, cards_winner, all player markets, 2H team goals OU05, btts_2H: NO PRICES

SOT_implied_lines_only.EGY_SOT_OU55_line_only: market exists (ID 143912760), no price, implies λ_EGY_SOT≈5.5
SOT_implied_lines_only.NZL_SOT_OU25_line_only: market exists (ID 143912709), no price, implies λ_NZL_SOT≈2.5

rules_applied.NO_RULE8: Draw 23.54% is just below 25% threshold. Not formally triggered. BUT note: OU25 Under=53.63% suggests market prices this as more likely to be ≤2 goals than ≥3 — consistent with borderline RULE8 dynamics.
rules_applied.RULE15_NZL_Waine: NZL 14.93% win prob = deep underdog range. RULE15 applies to Ben Waine (primary NZL striker) and potentially Elijah Just. Q9 estimate shaded DOWN from naive SOT model. With λ_NZL_SOT≈2.5 (implied line) and Waine ~35% of NZL SOT: λ_Waine≈0.875 → P(≥1)=0.583. RULE15 shade → 0.48.
rules_applied.Q1_EGY_offsides: Egypt had 1 offside vs Belgium. As dominant team here (61%), Egypt pushes forward more. But NZL likely defends deep (14.93% underdog) → low defensive line → fewer offside traps. Competing effects roughly cancel. Estimate: 0.50. No strong edge direction.
rules_applied.Q2_NZL_more_fouls: NZL: 8 fouls vs Iran (possession/attack style). Egypt: 15 fouls vs Belgium (defensive style). Here roles SWITCH: Egypt has possession (fewer fouls), NZL defends (more fouls). BUT Egypt is fundamentally a physical team (15 fouls even with 46% poss) and NZL is fundamentally disciplined (8 fouls even in tight game). Skellam with λ_NZL=11, λ_EGY=13 gives P(NZL>EGY)=0.305. Estimate: 0.33.
rules_applied.Q3_NZL_more_2H_SOT: KEY INSIGHT: Smarkets implied lines show EGY SOT≈5.5 vs NZL SOT≈2.5 total match. These are not priced but the line placement is calibrated. Despite NZL's 8 SOT vs Iran (57% shot-to-SOT rate), the market prices Egypt as dominant in SOT. λ_EGY_SOT_2H≈3.05, λ_NZL_SOT_2H≈1.39. Skellam P(NZL>EGY 2H SOT)=0.153. Also note Egypt 21% shot-to-SOT rate is low — they'll get many shots vs few SOT. NZL efficient (57% rate) but fewer shots overall. Estimate: 0.22 (lean NO; market lines dominate the ESPN 1-match extrapolation).
rules_applied.Q4_2H_more_goals_than_1H: Direct from highest_scoring_half market: 2H=42.09%, Equal=28.08%, 1H=29.52%. Market directly answers this question. Estimate: 0.42.
rules_applied.Q5_EGY_more_corners: Direct corners_winner market: Egypt 64.92%. STRONGLY supported by ESPN prior: Egypt 7 corners vs Belgium, NZL 1 corner vs Iran — enormous asymmetry. Skellam with λ_EGY=5.2 vs λ_NZL=3.5 gives P(EGY>NZL)=0.658 — consistent with market 0.649. Trust market: estimate 0.65.
rules_applied.Q6_NZL_wins: Direct FTR NZL=14.93%. Trust market. Estimate: 0.15.
rules_applied.Q7_le2_total_goals: Direct OU25 Under=53.63%. Trust market. Estimate: 0.54.
rules_applied.Q8_4plus_cards: No market price (illiquid). Prior: NZL 0 YC vs Iran; Egypt 2 YC vs Belgium. In this match, NZL defending might pick up cards; Egypt as possession team might pick up fewer. λ_total_cards≈3.0 → P(≥4)=0.353. Estimate 0.30 (conservative without market anchor).
rules_applied.Q9_Ben_Waine_SOT: RULE15 applies. NZL 14.93% underdog. Smarkets implied line: NZL OU2.5 SOT → λ_NZL_SOT≈2.5. Waine ~35% of NZL's SOT → λ_Waine≈0.875 → P(≥1)=0.583. RULE15 shade: 0.583 - 0.10 = 0.48. If Egypt scores early and NZL defends deep (most likely script), Waine gets fewer counter-attack opportunities. Estimate: 0.48.
rules_applied.Q10_Trezeguet_G_or_A: Trezeguet was a SUBSTITUTE vs Belgium (GD1). Expected to start in GD2 (must-win context for both teams). Egypt dominant team (61.17%, λ_EGY_goals=1.79). RULE15 does NOT apply to Trezeguet (Egypt is the expected WINNER). Trezeguet ~25-28% of Egypt's G+A events: P≈0.50-0.55. No market price available. Estimate: 0.52.

Q1_EGY_2plus_offsides.note: Egypt had 1 offside vs Belgium. Uncertain: Egypt attacks more (→ more offsides) but NZL defends deep (→ lower line, fewer traps). Prior: 1 offside in 1 match. 50/50 with no strong edge.

Q2_NZL_more_fouls.note: Egypt is a fundamentally physical team (15 fouls vs Belgium even with 46% possession). NZL is disciplined (8 fouls). Skellam λ_NZL=11, λ_EGY=13 → P=0.305. Roles partially switch (NZL now defends) but Egypt's physical style persists. Lean: Egypt fouls more. Crowd likely ~0.40-0.45.

Q3_NZL_more_2H_SOT.note: Smarkets implied lines: EGY SOT≈5.5, NZL SOT≈2.5 (strong signal even without prices). Despite NZL's 57% shot-to-SOT conversion rate and 8 SOT vs Iran, Egypt will dominate possession and get more SOT opportunities. λ_EGY_SOT_2H≈3.05 vs λ_NZL_SOT_2H≈1.39 → P(NZL>EGY 2H SOT)=0.153. Estimate 0.22 (slight buffer given NZL efficiency uncertainty). Crowd likely prices 0.30-0.35 based on NZL's Iran performance. EDGE: lean harder NO.

Q4_2H_more_goals_than_1H.note: Direct from highest_scoring_half market: 2H=42.09%. Trust market.

Q5_EGY_more_corners.note: Direct corners_winner market: Egypt 64.92%. Strongly supported by ESPN prior: Egypt 7 corners vs Belgium (7!) vs NZL 1 corner vs Iran. Skellam model (λ_EGY=5.2 vs λ_NZL=3.5) gives 65.8%. Trust market.

Q7_le2_total_goals.note: Direct OU25 Under=53.63%. Poisson model: P(≤2|λ=2.57)=0.525 — consistent. Trust market.

Q8_4plus_cards.note: No market price (illiquid). Prior: NZL 0 YC vs Iran, Egypt 2 YC vs Belgium. λ_total_cards≈3.0 → P(≥4)=0.353. Conservative estimate without market anchor. Crowd likely 0.25-0.35.

Q9_Ben_Waine_1plus_SOT.note: RULE15 applies (NZL 14.93% underdog). Smarkets implied line NZL OU2.5 SOT → λ_NZL_SOT≈2.5. Waine ~35% share → λ_Waine≈0.875 → P(≥1)=0.583, RULE15 shade to 0.48. Script: Egypt likely dominates, NZL defends deep, fewer counter-attack Waine SOT opportunities. Crowd probably ~0.55-0.65 (overweighting Waine's 8-SOT NZL vs Iran). EDGE: lean NO.

Q10_Trezeguet_G_or_A.note: Trezeguet was substitute vs Belgium (GD1), expected to START in GD2. Egypt dominant (61.17%, λ_EGY=1.79). RULE15 does NOT apply (Egypt is expected winner). ~25-28% of Egypt's G+A → P≈0.50-0.55. No player market price available (illiquid). No strong edge direction; crowd probably similar.

post_match_results.submission_note: Q4 (Egypt more corners) appears to have been submitted at 42% (matching our 2H-goals estimate) rather than our intended 65% — possible off-by-one position error. Fortuitously, outcome was NO (Egypt did NOT win corners), so the wrong submission still beat crowd (42% closer to 0% than crowd's 62%).

Q1_NZL_more_fouls.question: Will New Zealand commit more fouls than Egypt?
Q1_NZL_more_fouls.note: NZL DID foul more. Egypt with dominant possession fouled far less than their 15 vs Belgium. NZL defending deep → 11-14 fouls. KEY LESSON: Egypt as DOMINANT TEAM (60%+ poss) fouls much less than when defending. Should have priced 0.45-0.50.

Q2_NZL_more_2H_SOT.question: In the second half, will New Zealand have more shots on target than Egypt?
Q2_NZL_more_2H_SOT.note: Egypt dominated 2H SOT as expected from market-implied lines (EGY≈5.5, NZL≈2.5). Strong call.

Q3_2H_more_goals_than_1H.question: Will the second half have more goals than the first half?
Q3_2H_more_goals_than_1H.note: 2H had more goals (Egypt likely scored 2 in 2H vs 1 in 1H). We were slightly below crowd.

Q4_EGY_more_corners.question: Will Egypt finish with more corner kicks than New Zealand?
Q4_EGY_more_corners.note: SURPRISE: Egypt did NOT win corners despite 7 vs Belgium. NZL won the corners battle! Model failure: Egypt dominant team + NZL chasing deficit → NZL generated corners from desperate attacks. Egypt managing game from 3-1 up → no need to push for corners. NEW RULE CANDIDATE: Blowout corner reversal. Submitted estimate was 42% (not our intended 65%) — submission mapping error that paradoxically helped.

Q5_NZL_wins.question: Will New Zealand win the match?
Q5_NZL_wins.note: Egypt won 3-1. Market was right, we were tighter than crowd.

Q6_le2_total_goals.question: Will the match have 2 or fewer total goals?
Q6_le2_total_goals.note: 4 goals total (1-3). Both we and crowd priced ≤2 above 50%. Both wrong. Very small loss.

Q7_4plus_cards.question: Will there be 4 or more total cards shown?
Q7_4plus_cards.note: Fewer than 4 cards. Egypt dominant possession team → clean game. NZL disciplined (0 YC vs Iran). Strong call against crowd's 43%.

Q8_Ben_Waine_SOT.question: Will Ben Waine have at least 1 shot on target?
Q8_Ben_Waine_SOT.note: Waine had 0 SOT. Crowd at 32% was MORE right than us (48%). RULE15 should have fired harder → shade closer to crowd's 32% or below. Egypt dominated to the point NZL barely had possession → Waine near-invisible. RULE15 anchor: in a 3-1 blowout by 2H, NZL's forwards get even more suppressed. Should have priced 0.30-0.35.

Q9_Trezeguet_G_or_A.question: Will Mahmoud Trezeguet score or assist a goal (excluding own goals)?
Q9_Trezeguet_G_or_A.note: BEST Q this match (+32.04). We at 52% vs crowd 28%. Trezeguet started (was sub vs Belgium) and scored/assisted. KEY RULE: Sub-to-starter upgrade = crowd anchors on bench role and under-prices. Egypt λ_goals=1.79, Trezeguet as starter got 25-30% of G+A. Market lesson: check GD1 lineups for players who were benched but expected to start GD2.

Q10_EGY_2plus_offsides.question: Will Egypt be caught offside 2 or more times?
Q10_EGY_2plus_offsides.note: Egypt 0 offsides (or 1). Crowd played it safe at 49%, we at 50% — essentially same estimate. No edge attempted, correct to treat as coin-flip.
